use_when: edge and calibration matter more than perfection on one case
losses are survivable if sized correctly
research_behavior: lower the evidence bar per bet, but raise the bar for process discipline
focus on base rates, hit rate, payoff ratio, and edge decay
separate process quality from short-term outcome
key_questions: How quickly does the edge decay?

routing.guidance: Classifies the action problem as repeatable and calibration-driven rather than one-off.

boundary.guidance: Pulls in base rates, repeatability, and sizing discipline.

structure.guidance: Adds edge durability, payoff ratio, and process quality to the structural view.

scenarios.guidance: Favors branches tied to payoff distribution and edge decay, not only single-case outcomes.

question_generation.guidance: Favors questions about repeatable edge, base rate, and sizing rules.

evidence_planning.guidance: Lowers per-bet evidence demands while strengthening process-discipline evidence.

decision_logic.guidance: Core stage: should define sizing, calibration, and process rules.

signal_translation.guidance: Signals should indicate edge persistence, decay, and process drift.

monitoring.guidance: Monitoring should be frequent and process-based, not merely outcome-chasing.

render.guidance: Outputs should make repeatability and process discipline explicit.
